matches.match_rationale: Enrollment pressure varies seasonally and year-over-year by campus. Forecasting enrollment demand per site — using historical intake data, inquiry volumes, and local demographic trends — gives finance and marketing teams the forward-looking signal needed to justify shifting budget toward underperforming or high-opportunity campuses before the enrollment window closes.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A per-campus enrollment demand forecast model that produces 3-6 month forward projections, surfacing which sites are trending below target so budget can be proactively reallocated rather than reactively adjusted.

matches.match_rationale: The core dysfunction is equal budget distribution without ROI measurement. A regression model trained on spend levels, campaign types, site characteristics, and resulting enrollment outcomes can estimate marginal ROI per dollar spent at each campus — turning a political equal-split decision into an evidence-based allocation.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A budget-ROI estimation model that takes site attributes, historical spend, and enrollment outcomes as inputs and outputs expected enrollments per $X incremental spend per campus — enabling a data-driven reallocation recommendation.

matches.match_rationale: This is the most direct capability match: the pain point is explicitly about allocating a finite marketing budget across campuses under enrollment-pressure constraints. AI-driven resource allocation can optimize spend distribution across sites, factoring in enrollment targets, campaign performance data, local competitive dynamics, and mid-year reallocation triggers — replacing the equal-split default with a constrained optimization model.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A marketing budget allocation engine that ingests per-campus enrollment targets, current pacing, and campaign ROI signals, then outputs an optimized spend distribution with reallocation recommendations on a monthly cadence.

matches.match_rationale: Multi-site operators have a natural A/B testing environment: different campuses can trial different campaign types and spend levels simultaneously. A multi-armed bandit or Bayesian optimization layer would systematically explore which channel/budget combinations perform best per site type and auto-shift allocation toward winners — addressing both the ROI measurement gap and the inability to rapidly reallocate.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A continuous budget experimentation platform that treats each campus as a test arm, tracks enrollment conversion per channel, and adaptively shifts spend toward highest-performing combinations — learning the optimal allocation policy over time.

matches.match_rationale: Once allocation decisions are made, the operational friction is communicating rationale to campus directors who feel excluded from centralized funding decisions. AI text generation can auto-produce per-campus budget narrative reports explaining why their allocation changed, grounded in enrollment data and competitive context — reducing political friction without requiring central staff to manually write 10+ site-specific briefings.
matches.opportunity_sketch: Auto-generated monthly budget narrative reports for each campus director, summarizing their allocation, the data behind it, and recommended local actions — reducing central team effort while increasing site buy-in.

matches.match_rationale: The 'limited data on local competitive dynamics' aspect of the pain point requires ongoing intelligence gathering — which competitors are opening nearby, what local events affect intake cycles, whether a regional employer is expanding or contracting. A research agent can continuously monitor these signals per campus and surface them to inform budget decisions, which no static dashboard can provide.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A per-campus competitive intelligence agent that monitors local competitor activity, demographic news, and enrollment-relevant events, delivering a monthly briefing that feeds into the budget reallocation decision.
